steps.title: Block the form (pencil)
steps.description: Lightly sketch a rounded shape — a little wider than tall, gently asymmetric. Real apples are never perfect circles, so let yours lean a touch to one side.

steps.title: Mark the top and bottom (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, add a small inward dip at the top of the outline where the stem will sit, and a shallower matching curve at the bottom. These are placement guides, not final lines.

steps.title: Switch to ink and trace the outline
steps.description: Switch to ink. Trace the apple's silhouette in one confident pass, following your pencil guide. Don't try to match it exactly — let your hand pick the cleanest path. A slight wobble reads as ripe and organic.

steps.title: Ink the stem and leaf (ink)
steps.description: Still in ink, add a short stem rising from the top dip, tilted slightly to one side. Then sketch a small leaf next to it with two strokes meeting at a point, and a single central vein down its middle.

steps.title: Suggest the stem well (ink)
steps.description: Add a small curved line just inside the top dip to suggest the little well where the stem sits. One short stroke is plenty.
